clause: Content Blocks section 3; Anchors and References section 4 (range semantics); Presentation Layers; Asset Embedding section 8 — structural invariants JSON Schema cannot express
description: Content-tree and file-level invariants JSON Schema cannot express: upward containment, figure/definitionItem cardinality, half-open anchor ranges, precise-layout page-number uniqueness, asset-index count/totalSize consistency, and id uniqueness. Each case is self-describing — block-tree cases ship the block-type set their fire/clean outcome depends on, so no schema-derived state is required.
oracle: Expected valid/invalid outcomes are HAND-DETERMINED from the specification’s structural rules (a human reasoning about upward containment, cardinality, half-open ranges, and uniqueness), never snapshotted. The reference checkers (scripts/lib/structural-constraints.ts) reproduce every outcome and also back the check:structural gate.

vectors.name: upward-figcaption-at-document-root
vectors.description: A figcaption at the document root violates upward containment (its parent must be a figure).

vectors.name: upward-tablecell-under-paragraph
vectors.description: A tableCell whose nearest block parent is a paragraph (not a tableRow) violates containment; it fires only because paragraph is recognized as a block, giving a concrete non-excerpt parent.

vectors.name: upward-tablerow-at-document-root
vectors.description: A tableRow at the document root violates containment (its parent must be a table).

vectors.name: upward-figcaption-under-figure
vectors.description: A figcaption directly under a figure is correctly contained.

vectors.name: upward-standalone-figcaption-excerpt
vectors.description: A figcaption at an EXCERPT root is exempt (its real parent is unknown), so containment does not fire.

vectors.name: figure-two-content-blocks
vectors.description: A figure (children form) with two content blocks violates the exactly-one-content rule.

vectors.name: figure-no-content-block
vectors.description: A figure whose only child is a figcaption has zero content blocks — invalid.

vectors.name: figure-two-figcaptions
vectors.description: A figure with two figcaptions exceeds the at-most-one-caption rule.

vectors.name: figure-one-content-one-caption
vectors.description: A figure with one content block and one figcaption satisfies the cardinality rule.

vectors.name: figure-using-subfigures
vectors.description: A figure using the subfigures multi-panel form is a distinct construct with its own arity — the children-form cardinality does not apply.

vectors.name: definitionitem-two-terms-no-description
vectors.description: A definitionItem with two terms and no description lacks the required description.

vectors.name: definitionitem-term-and-description
vectors.description: A definitionItem with one term and one description satisfies the cardinality rule.

vectors.name: anchor-object-inverted-range
vectors.description: A range anchor object with start > end (10 > 5) violates the half-open interval rule.

vectors.name: anchor-object-empty-range
vectors.description: A range anchor with start == end (7,7) is empty; a half-open interval requires start < end.

vectors.name: anchor-uri-inverted-range
vectors.description: A content-anchor URI #intro/10-5 has start not less than end.

vectors.name: anchor-object-valid-range
vectors.description: A range anchor object with start < end (1,2) is valid.

vectors.name: anchor-uri-valid-range
vectors.description: A content-anchor URI #intro/10-25 with start < end is valid.

vectors.name: anchor-block-level-no-range
vectors.description: A block-level anchor carrying no start/end is not a range anchor and is not subject to the rule.

vectors.name: pages-duplicate-numbers
vectors.description: A precise layout with two pages numbered 1 violates page-number uniqueness.

vectors.name: pages-unique-numbers
vectors.description: A precise layout with distinct page numbers (1,2) is valid.

vectors.name: asset-duplicate-ids
vectors.description: Two assets sharing an id in one index violate asset-id uniqueness.

vectors.name: asset-count-mismatch
vectors.description: A category count of 2 with a single asset in the index is inconsistent. SHOULD-level: the manifest count/totalSize fields are advisory and their exact semantics are not normatively defined, so a mismatch is reported, never fatal.

vectors.name: asset-totalsize-ignores-variant
vectors.description: A category totalSize of 10 that omits a variant’s size (asset 10 + variant 5 = 15) is inconsistent under the assets+variants reading. SHOULD-level: totalSize is advisory and its summation rule is not normatively defined.

vectors.name: asset-consistent-with-variant
vectors.description: A category totalSize of 15 summing the asset (10) and its variant (5) is consistent under the assets+variants reading. SHOULD-level: an implementation using a different (also-permitted) totalSize reading is not failed.

vectors.name: ids-duplicate
vectors.description: Two entries sharing an id in one list violate id uniqueness. Generic structural invariant over any id-bearing items array (the case ships no extension-specific structure), so it is core.

vectors.name: ids-unique
vectors.description: Entries with distinct ids in a list satisfy id uniqueness (generic structural invariant over id-bearing items).
